page: Amazon Web Services m6a.2xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor operating at a speed of 3.6 GHz. The configuration provides 8 dedicated vCPUs across 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It includes 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, translating to 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance type, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps.

In benchmark testing, the instance demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 25% for stress-ng single-core operations. Multi-core CPU performance, Geekbench scores, and Passmark CPU metrics fall within the average range. However, memory bandwidth for large, uncached blocks is relatively weak, placing in the bottom 25% for both read and write operations. Similarly, while small-model LLM inference and text generation perform at average speeds, prompt processing for medium-sized 7B models is weak.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m6a.2xlarge offers a balanced resource density of 4 GB of RAM per vCPU, typical of general-purpose hardware classes. It provides a stable, dedicated compute profile without the premium cost of memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated instances. This makes it suitable for general-purpose workloads such as web serving, database operations, redis caching, and software compilation, though it is less optimal for memory-intensive streaming or large-scale LLM inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m6a.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.6 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. Managed by the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it delivers 8 dedicated vCPUs and 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory. The instance features no local storage or GPUs, relying on network-attached storage and offering a 3 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks show strong single-core CPU performance, though large-block memory bandwidth and medium-model LLM prompt processing are weak. Most other metrics, including multi-core CPU, database operations, and web serving throughput, perform in the average tier. This balanced resource profile makes the m6a.2xlarge a cost-efficient choice for general-purpose workloads like web serving, application hosting, and moderate database tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6a.2xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose instance featuring 8 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 32 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 3 Gbps network baseline, delivering balanced compute and memory performance.

tagline: A balanced x86_64 virtual instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and DDR4 memory for general-purpose computing workloads.

Equipped with 32 GB of DDR4 memory at 3 GHz
Strong single-core CPU performance in benchmark testing
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Well-suited for web serving, application hosting, and database operations
